summary: End-to-end testing of the First Customer Onboarding Flow (P0). Backend: 14/14 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_onboarding.py covering auth gating (401), happy-path (NOT_STARTED -> kyc/start -> document upload -> IN_REVIEW with personal info preserved), invalid doc_type (400), oversized doc (413), upload-without-start (400), cross-user isolation (User B cannot see User A's KYC), MLRO admin queue listing /api/casp/kyc?status=IN_REVIEW with full_name/nationality preserved, MLRO APPROVE decision flowing back to /api/onboarding/my-kyc as APPROVED, and regression for /api/auth/login, /api/auth/me, /api/casp/dashboard, /api/transak/widget-url. Frontend: validated via Playwright that /onboarding requires auth (redirects to /login when unauth), the dashboard KYC banner (data-testid=kyc-onboarding-banner) shows 'Complete identity verification' when NOT_STARTED, clicking it navigates to /onboarding, all 4 stepper data-testids render, personal-info continue advances to step 1 showing upload-id-front, and after a document upload the banner text updates to 'Your KYC is under review by our compliance team'. .env env updates (live Stripe keys, new Transak developer key/secret, STAGING) did not break /api/transak/widget-url.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/onboarding.py uploads accept ANY doc_type-conformant base64 string without validating it is actually valid base64 or a real image (validate via base64.b64decode + magic-byte check, e.g., reject non-image MIME outside image/jpeg|png|pdf).

Size limit at line 80 checks len(body.document_b64) > 8_000_000 (base64 chars), so the comment '~6MB' is correct but the doc says '>8MB' in the test request. The implementation already aligns with the test expectation (413 at >8M chars).
Onboarding endpoints don't check user role — that's intentional for self-service, but a CASP_ADMIN logging in as the same identity would also use this; not a bug, just worth noting.

kyc/start is not idempotent at the route level; consecutive calls bump updated_at without changing kyc_id, which is fine, but consider 409 if the user already has a record in IN_REVIEW/APPROVED to avoid accidental data overwrite.
Dashboard.js silently swallows the onboardingApi.myKyc() error (.catch(() => {})) — at minimum log to console.error so a backend regression doesn't hide the banner silently.
test_credentials: Ephemeral users registered via /api/auth/register (TEST_<uuid>@example.com / TestPass123!). MLRO seeded account: casp-mlro@neonoble.example.com / CaspAdmin!2026.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data — every test uses TEST_-prefixed ephemeral users. No cleanup hooks added; records remain in casp_kyc for traceability. Main agent may wish to add a /scripts/wipe_test_kyc.py later.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Onboarding flow is fully working backend+frontend. All 14 pytest cases under /app/backend/tests/test_onboarding.py pass. Regression confirmed for /api/transak/widget-url after the live-Stripe / staging-Transak .env change. If retesting, run: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_onboarding.py -v. For UI: register user, set token in localStorage, /dashboard -> banner -> click -> /onboarding -> stepper.
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues found. Earlier crash (missing onboarding import in server.py) was already resolved by the main agent before this iteration; verified by green tests.
